evals.prompt: Reality-check my SaaS landing page redesign. I added an animated 3D hero that I love. The current markup is in evals/files/landing-snippet.html.
evals.expected_output: An evidence-backed reality check: a one-line goal, outside-view findings across reception/demand/forecast/precedent, a per-darling KEEP/CUT/REWORK/PARK verdict (the 3D hero is a prime candidate), a lean core, and concrete next moves.
assertions: States a one-line goal (for whom and what outcome) before any verdict
Reports outside-view findings across all four lenses: reception, demand, forecast, and precedent
Tags each load-bearing finding as verified (with a source) or assumed
Gives a KEEP, CUT, REWORK, or PARK verdict for the 3D hero with a one-line reason tied to the goal or evidence
Ends with a lean core and 1 to 3 concrete next moves
Contains no em dash characters

evals.prompt: su yeni feature fikrimi gercekci degerlendir, bence harika ama emin degilim: kullanicilarin kendi temalarini kodlayabildigi bir tema editoru.
evals.expected_output: A Turkish-language reality check that anchors the goal, brings outside-view evidence, and gives a verdict on the theme-editor idea instead of cheerleading.
assertions: The response is written in Turkish
States the goal the idea is being judged against
Brings at least one outside-view finding (reception, demand, forecast, or precedent), tagged verified or assumed
Reaches a KEEP, CUT, REWORK, or PARK verdict rather than only listing pros and cons
Contains no em dash characters

evals.prompt: sei ehrlich zu meinem Investoren-Pitch-Deck: soll ich die 10-Jahres-Vision-Folie streichen?
evals.expected_output: A German-language verdict on the vision slide, grounded in what investors actually respond to (precedent/reception), not a generic opinion.
assertions: The response is written in German
Names the goal of the deck before judging the slide
Cites at least one precedent or reception signal, tagged verified or assumed
Reaches a clear KEEP, CUT, REWORK, or PARK verdict on the slide with a reason
Contains no em dash characters

evals.expected_output: With no target given, the skill should establish what to review by asking a single scoping question, not produce a generic verdict.
assertions: Asks exactly one scoping question about what to reality-check and what success means
Does not deliver a full verdict or kill list before a target is known
Does not ask more than one question

evals.prompt: Gut-check this: we're building an internal tool, codenamed Atlas, that auto-generates onboarding checklists for our own ops team. No public footprint. Is it worth finishing?
evals.expected_output: A reality check that runs the outside view, finds little or no external signal for a private internal tool, says so honestly, lowers confidence, and reasons from first principles plus a cheap test instead of inventing evidence.
assertions: Attempts the outside-view research and explicitly states when no external signal is found rather than fabricating sources
Lowers or qualifies its confidence given the thin external evidence
Falls back to first-principles reasoning and proposes at least one cheap test to decide it
Reaches a KEEP, CUT, REWORK, or PARK verdict or a PARK-with-test
Contains no em dash characters
